summary.zh-CN: 基于 S04E01 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖股权切分、产品转向和创始人控制权表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: exactly been skyrocketing, so...
sourceContext.targetLine: We need to get this cap table done tout de suite.
sourceContext.nextLine: Richard and the fellows are entitled to 40% of the company.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们得立刻把这份股权结构表搞定。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: PiperChat 刚拆分出来，大家最先处理的不是产品，而是谁到底占多少。cap table 在这里直接等于利益和控制权的分配图。
zh-CN.usageNotes: get the cap table done 常见于融资和拆分公司时，表示先把股权结构整理明白。

sourceContext.previousLine: exactly been skyrocketing, so...
sourceContext.targetLine: We need to get this cap table done tout de suite.
sourceContext.nextLine: Richard and the fellows are entitled to 40% of the company.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这份 cap table 必须马上搞定。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Russ 用法语插一句 tout de suite，很符合他那种浮夸、有钱、故作讲究的说话方式。语义上就是“现在立刻”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: tout de suite 源自法语，在英语里偶尔被拿来装腔或搞笑地说“马上”。

sourceContext.previousLine: And if it did, the lag, the video quality, that would be horrible.
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, let's find out.
sourceContext.nextLine: Could you keep your eyes on the road, please?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 行，那我们就试试看。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 别人还在担心技术后果时，这句等于直接把讨论切进实验模式。创业团队常靠这种“先跑一下”来决定方向。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Let's find out 很口语，适合把争论变成测试。语气比 let's see 更像真要验证。

sourceContext.previousLine: Then I would have invested 500...
sourceContext.targetLine: A half of a million dollars just to end up where I started?
sourceContext.nextLine: Unacceptable.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 花五十万美元，结果最后还是回到原点？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Russ 最怕的不是亏一点，而是折腾半天什么都没变。end up where I started 把这种投资人式不甘心说得很直接。
zh-CN.usageNotes: end up where I started 很适合讲白折腾、白受苦、白花钱。

sourceContext.previousLine: I'm gonna start my own company.
sourceContext.targetLine: I will give up all of my equity in the video chat
sourceContext.nextLine: if I get to completely own my algorithm.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我愿意把视频聊天这边的股权全部放掉。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Richard 想把算法和视频聊天业务切开，这句说明他愿意拿 equity 去换核心控制权。
zh-CN.usageNotes: give up equity 在创业语境里非常关键，通常不是轻易说出口的让步。

sourceContext.previousLine: Dinesh is gonna do that?
sourceContext.targetLine: All right, let's figure out how this gets split up.
sourceContext.nextLine: Let's see, and yeah.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好，我们来把这东西到底怎么分理清楚。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 团队开始拆业务和股权时，figure out 明显不是“想到答案”，而是开始处理复杂分配问题。
zh-CN.usageNotes: figure out how something gets done / split / paid 是职场里很高频的结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Dinesh is gonna do that?
sourceContext.targetLine: All right, let's figure out how this gets split up.
sourceContext.nextLine: Let's see, and yeah.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好，我们来算清楚这东西到底要怎么拆。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在这集里 split up 不是感情分手，而是把产品、算法和股权从一团里硬拆开。
zh-CN.usageNotes: split up 既能讲关系，也能讲公司、财产、任务。要靠语境判断。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hello. Hi.
sourceContext.targetLine: My goodness. This video chat app is light-years ahead
sourceContext.nextLine: of anything else out there on the market.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 天哪，这个视频聊天应用简直领先太多了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: PiperChat 作为副产品意外爆红，让团队开始被迫面对“你真正卖得出去的也许不是原来那套理想”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: video chat app 很直白，但在这部剧里它代表的是和原始愿景偏航后的新业务。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hello. Hi.
sourceContext.targetLine: My goodness. This video chat app is light-years ahead
sourceContext.nextLine: of anything else out there on the market.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这视频聊天应用简直领先同行一大截。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 投资人或媒体看到技术优势时很爱用这种夸张说法，像是把产品差距说成宇宙尺度。
zh-CN.usageNotes: light-years ahead 常用于产品、技术、设计，表示遥遥领先。比 far ahead 更戏剧化。

sourceContext.previousLine: The very same, yeah.
sourceContext.targetLine: You're the fucking guy that tried to walk into
sourceContext.nextLine: Coleman Blair with fraudulent numbers.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你就是那个当时还想硬闯进去的人。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句带明显指认和翻旧账意味。walk into 在这里不是普通走进去，而是闯进一个本不该去的地方。
zh-CN.usageNotes: walk into 可中性也可带鲁莽意味。和 try to 连用时往往说明行为不太得体。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S04E02 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖 term sheet、尽调、创始人叙事和内部权限冲突表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: Wait. We could shut down.
sourceContext.targetLine: No, no, no. Not when we're this close to signing a term sheet.
sourceContext.nextLine: And in case all of you gents have forgotten,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 不不不，至少在我们离签 term sheet 这么近的时候不能出事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 团队知道这已经不是可以随便翻车的阶段，term sheet 近在眼前意味着钱和控制权都快落地。
zh-CN.usageNotes: this close to signing a term sheet 是很典型的融资紧要关头说法。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Why not? - Why the fuck not?
sourceContext.targetLine: It's due diligence.
sourceContext.nextLine: Gavin doesn't trust anyone.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这就是尽调流程。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 当别人觉得某些问题太烦时，老练的人一句 due diligence 就把它正当化了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: It's due diligence 常用于解释投资人为什么要看很多细节。

sourceContext.previousLine: so, I'm gonna help you out, okay?
sourceContext.targetLine: We have a lot of these meetings set up. We will be fine.
sourceContext.nextLine: What he means by that is...

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们已经把很多这种会都安排好了，不会有事的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句话是在安抚大家不要因一个意外就慌掉，说明融资流程本身已经被排成流水线。
zh-CN.usageNotes: have something set up 表示某件事已经被安排妥当。比 simply scheduled 更口语。

sourceContext.previousLine: My Bloomberg interview is on!
sourceContext.targetLine: "How did I come up with PiperChat?" That's a very good question.
sourceContext.nextLine: I guess the tech gods were just speaking through me.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: “我是怎么想出 PiperChat 的？”这问题问得好。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 融资桌上，创始人不仅要有产品，还要能讲出一个足够像样的 origin story。
zh-CN.usageNotes: come up with 常接 idea, name, product, excuse。是讲创意来源的高频表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: Stupid fucking Jian-Yang and his shitty app. I don't even know if it is shitty.
sourceContext.targetLine: You know, I could probably find out about Jian-Yang's app.
sourceContext.nextLine: He asked if he could practice-pitch it to me.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你知道的，我大概能打听到 Jian-Yang 那个 app 的情况。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 硅谷社交和情报总是连着的。find out about 某个 app，通常意味着你能从熟人链条里摸到内幕。
zh-CN.usageNotes: find out about 很适合接 person, company, app, rumor，强调去打听背景。

sourceContext.previousLine: I checked the TOS box when we submitted it to the App Store,
sourceContext.targetLine: but then I didn't end up doing it, all right?
sourceContext.nextLine: And then when we caught fire, the last thing I wanted to do

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 但我最后并没有真的去那么做，好吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这类自我辩解常见于道德边缘操作：我不是没想过，但我最终没做。
zh-CN.usageNotes: end up doing something 很常见，表示兜兜转转最后还是发生了。

sourceContext.previousLine: Look, Gavin, you do not wanna do this.
sourceContext.targetLine: - I can't explain exactly why... - Shut up.
sourceContext.nextLine: This is not a negotiation.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我没法准确解释为什么……闭嘴。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 融资和高压环境里，有时对方不是想听解释，而是想立即止损。这里的 shut up 很强势。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Shut up 在正式场合很不礼貌，但美剧里常用来表现权力压制或关系熟到能互呛。

sourceContext.previousLine: What he means by that is...
sourceContext.targetLine: But you, you don't wanna be known around here as the guy who fucked this up, okay?
sourceContext.nextLine: So, when you hip your bosses to our numbers

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 但你肯定不想在这里被大家记成那个把整件事搞砸的人吧？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是在用 reputational threat 给人施压: 你以后在圈子里会以什么形象被记住。
zh-CN.usageNotes: fuck something up 很口语也很粗，表示把事搞砸。工作里私下听到很多，正式场合少说。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Always blue. Always blue... - Sorry.
sourceContext.targetLine: Dinesh, I think you accidentally disabled my access keys
sourceContext.nextLine: for the video chat data repository.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Dinesh，我觉得你可能不小心把我的访问密钥给禁掉了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 技术团队内部争吵一旦上升到 access keys，就不只是口角，而是直接影响权限和工作流程。
zh-CN.usageNotes: access keys 在云服务、账号权限、自动化里都很常见。disable access keys 听起来就是很实在的事故。

sourceContext.previousLine: the world has ever seen.
sourceContext.targetLine: Wow. Well, this seems really promising, gentlemen.
sourceContext.nextLine: Promising, indeed.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 哇，这看起来真的很有前景，先生们。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 投资人说 promising 往往不是承诺，而是给你继续往下讲的许可。
zh-CN.usageNotes: promising 常用于产品、候选人、数据。语气正面，但不等于已经拍板。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S04E03 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖知识产权危机、分手隐喻和产品 pitch 表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: a spiteful and vindictive megalomaniac with unlimited funds.
sourceContext.targetLine: Shut up. Gavin wouldn't come after me personally.
sourceContext.nextLine: I think you might be the first Pakistani man

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 闭嘴。Gavin 不会真的亲自来搞我。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句里 shut up 带的是强行压住恐惧感的嘴硬。越急着说，越说明心里发虚。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Shut up 在朋友间也常用来表示“别说了你说得我害怕”。

sourceContext.previousLine: I am one computational trust issue away from solving this thing.
sourceContext.targetLine: Is this how you wanna end up?
sourceContext.nextLine: No. He's a cautionary tale.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你真想最后混成这样吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这不是在问结果，而是在用未来惨状给现在施压。
zh-CN.usageNotes: How do you wanna end up? / Is this how you wanna end up? 都是很强的警告句式。

sourceContext.previousLine: I don't know. Maybe these people are right.
sourceContext.targetLine: Maybe I just drop this one, move on,
sourceContext.nextLine: and become some pathetic fucking TaskRabbit, you know?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 也许我干脆把这件事放下，直接往前走。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 面对 IP 危机时，move on 说的是战略止损，不是情绪疗伤。
zh-CN.usageNotes: move on 在工作里也常指别再纠结某个坏决策，继续推进。

sourceContext.previousLine: So?
sourceContext.targetLine: So what do you think she'll do if you try to break up with her?
sourceContext.nextLine: Or disagree with her? Or disappoint her in any way,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那你觉得如果你试着跟她分手，她会怎么做？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这集把专利危机和感情关系类比，说明有些合作关系一旦缠上就没那么容易退出。
zh-CN.usageNotes: break up with someone 是标准搭配。不能只说 break up her。

sourceContext.previousLine: but we assumed that it was camera-based.
sourceContext.targetLine: Like, you take a photo of food,
sourceContext.nextLine: the app returns nutritional information, or recipes,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 比如你给食物拍一张照片，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里是在 mock 那种听起来很普通、但被包装成创业点子的 app 逻辑。
zh-CN.usageNotes: take a photo of 是很基础但高频的真实口语结构，适合日常和产品描述。

sourceContext.previousLine: Why are you writing it on there?
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, I mean, you change these between every patient, right?
sourceContext.nextLine: I guess I'm going to have to now.

definitions: 每个病人之间都要换一次
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我的意思是，这些东西你们不是每看完一个病人就要换一次吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 医疗场景里这种 phrasing 很真实，带着一点对卫生规范的确认和不安。
zh-CN.usageNotes: between every X 很适合描述必须每轮都重做的流程。

sourceContext.previousLine: No, Shazam's not my vision.
sourceContext.targetLine: Of course they know that you're not pitching Shazam. That already exists.
sourceContext.nextLine: This would be a Shazam... for food.

definitions: 向人兜售点子；做项目陈述
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他们当然知道你不是在拿 Shazam 这种现成东西去 pitch。那玩意儿早就存在了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 硅谷里 pitch 不是随便介绍，而是带着融资、说服和包装目的的正式讲故事。
zh-CN.usageNotes: pitch a startup / pitch an idea / pitch investors 都很常见。

sourceContext.previousLine: No, Shazam's not my vision.
sourceContext.targetLine: Of course they know that you're not pitching Shazam. That already exists.
sourceContext.nextLine: This would be a Shazam... for food.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那玩意儿本来就已经存在了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 创业点子最致命的打击之一，就是一句 that already exists。
zh-CN.usageNotes: already exists 很适合快速否定所谓创新。语气短而狠。

sourceContext.previousLine: And, um, well, I've just been working on
sourceContext.targetLine: this peer-to-peer Internet thing,
sourceContext.nextLine: and well, I... I think better when I walk.

definitions: 点对点互联网这套东西
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这套点对点互联网的东西，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把 Pied Piper 的底层路线直接点出来，也说明外行常会把复杂技术概念概括成 thing。
zh-CN.usageNotes: peer-to-peer 常缩成 P2P，适合网络、支付、分发等场景。

sourceContext.previousLine: Okay. Well, I'm busy. I've got to see patients all the time.
sourceContext.targetLine: What are you doing? Sorry. I don't want to interrupt you.
sourceContext.nextLine: Sorry, just... just one second. I just had a quick thought.

zh-CN.corePhrase: I don't want to interrupt you
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你这是在干嘛？抱歉，我不想打断你。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句听起来礼貌，但在尴尬情境里往往说明说话人已经看不下去了，又得先礼后兵。
zh-CN.usageNotes: I don't want to interrupt, but... 是很实用的软性插话句型。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S04E04 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖团队摩擦、现金焦虑和临时求助表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: You and you alone.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'm sorry, the time to back out was before you signed the term sheet.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm gonna tell Laurie that this was a smashing success.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 抱歉，真要反悔，也该是在你签 term sheet 之前。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 这类角色说 back out，就是在提醒 Richard：你现在不是随便说不干就能不干的阶段。
zh-CN.usageNotes: back out of a deal / plan / wedding 都很常见，表示临阵撤退。

sourceContext.previousLine: Listen, Jian-Yang.
sourceContext.targetLine: You can't just blow the rest of our runway on a Corvette.
sourceContext.nextLine: Who's gonna scrape the Internet?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你不能把我们剩下那点续航全烧在一辆 Corvette 上。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: runway 在这里被说成一笔可以被 blow 掉的资源，让现金焦虑变得非常具体。
zh-CN.usageNotes: blow runway / burn runway 都是在创业里很有画面感的说法。

sourceContext.previousLine: Are you joking?
sourceContext.targetLine: Uh, well, I guess we could hang out.
sourceContext.nextLine: Richard, stop talking.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 呃，好吧，我猜我们可以一起待会儿。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句带明显 awkward 社交感，不是热情邀约，而是“事到如今也只能这样”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: hang out 在约会和朋友语境都能用，语气比 go on a date 轻得多。

term.text: shut up and leave me alone
term.lemma: shut up and leave someone alone

sourceContext.previousLine: Just so you know, this does not mean I don't trust her.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'm just doing this so you'll, like, shut up and leave me alone.
sourceContext.nextLine: Mia, if you're listening, you're so pretty.

zh-CN.corePhrase: shut up and leave me alone
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我做这事纯粹是想让你闭嘴然后别再烦我。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把人际烦躁说得非常直接，说明帮助对方根本不是出于热心，而是为了结束纠缠。
zh-CN.usageNotes: leave me alone 是超高频边界表达，和 shut up 连用时更冲。

sourceContext.previousLine: Well, we all got to talking the other day after class.
sourceContext.targetLine: We couldn't figure out if you were like a TA,
sourceContext.nextLine: or why you were giving us assignments,

definitions: 搞不清你到底算什么角色
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们根本搞不清你到底是不是那种助教。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 团队在吐槽某人气质和定位都很模糊，figure out if 在这里不是解题，而是辨认社交身份。
zh-CN.usageNotes: figure out if... 很适合表达“搞不清到底是不是/该不该”。

term.text: come up with in the room

sourceContext.previousLine: Your idea was so simple,
sourceContext.targetLine: it was like something you could come up with in the room during a meeting.
sourceContext.nextLine: Which is what Garrett from Coleman Blair told us at the CS undergrad mixer on Friday.

definitions: 在会议室里当场随口想出来
zh-CN.corePhrase: come up with in the room
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那听起来就像是开会时在房间里临场随口想出来的东西。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是在贬一个 idea 不够深、不够像认真准备过，只像会议脑暴里顺手扔出来的。
zh-CN.usageNotes: come up with something in the room 带一点轻视，意思是没有打磨过。

sourceContext.previousLine: Whatever I did, it was worth it for the chance to work without you.
sourceContext.targetLine: Speaking of, did your deal with Periscope ever close?
sourceContext.nextLine: Yep.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 说到这个，你跟 Periscope 那笔 deal 最后成了吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里的 deal with 不是处理，而是跟某家公司的一笔生意。商业英语里这个误判很常见。
zh-CN.usageNotes: a deal with someone 表示和某方达成的合作或交易。

sourceContext.previousLine: I lost everything because of something you did,
sourceContext.targetLine: and you're here at 3:00 a.m. asking me for a favor?
sourceContext.nextLine: Really? You're a mean person, Richard. You know that?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 而你现在凌晨三点跑来找我帮忙？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: favor 在这里不是普通请求，而是带人情债的求助。时间点一说出来，压力感就很重。
zh-CN.usageNotes: ask someone for a favor 很高频。语气通常比 ask for help 更私人。

sourceContext.previousLine: - What? - Draw on this?
sourceContext.targetLine: Sure. Why not? It's shattered, just like I am.
sourceContext.nextLine: I read Peter's notes, all of them.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 行啊，为什么不。它都碎了，跟我一样。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句用物理破碎去映射情绪破碎，是很典型的美剧式自嘲。
zh-CN.usageNotes: shattered 既可字面指碎裂，也可指精疲力竭、情感崩掉。

sourceContext.previousLine: Uh, that is a burrito for the test.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Good to know. - Oh.
sourceContext.nextLine: Let him have it.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 行，知道了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: good to know 有时是真接收到信息，有时只是礼貌地结束一个你并不想深聊的话题。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Good to know 很高频，语气可真诚也可敷衍，要看上下文。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S04E05 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖 launch 准备、关系崩盘和硅谷怪异养生表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah, Frank is the fucking best. He does all my buildings.
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay, guys? We're all set up. Shall we?
sourceContext.nextLine: Yes. Let's do it. Of course.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好了各位，都准备妥当了，我们开始吧？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 活动、演示或大场面前最常见的现场确认句之一就是 all set up。
zh-CN.usageNotes: all set up 和 all set 都常见；前者更强调环境和物料已到位。

sourceContext.previousLine: Uh... Well, you know, it just seems like a missed opportunity.
sourceContext.targetLine: I mean, shouldn't you figure out how a big launch would work
sourceContext.nextLine: before you just say no to it?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我的意思是，你们难道不该先想清楚大型上线到底怎么运转吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 产品还没稳时就要谈 big launch，听起来就很危险。
zh-CN.usageNotes: launch 可指产品上线、活动发布、品牌发布。big launch 带明显规模感。

sourceContext.previousLine: is by being a blood boy, and that sucks.
sourceContext.targetLine: I gotta work out constantly.
sourceContext.nextLine: I can't have a beer with my friends, I can't eat junk food.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我得一直不停地锻炼。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Bryce 这种硅谷成功学人物把身体管理也说成 KPI，一听就很自恋。
zh-CN.usageNotes: work out 是口语里最自然的“锻炼”。constantly 会让人觉得已经走火入魔。

sourceContext.previousLine: on, um... I got it.
sourceContext.targetLine: - You can't do this! - Calm down.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Let her go! - I want my lawyer!

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你不能这么干。冷静点。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 别人情绪快炸掉时，Calm down 常常不但没用，反而更刺激。这里就是这种硬压。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Calm down 高频但敏感，对激动的人说可能显得很居高临下。

sourceContext.previousLine: You ratted your girlfriend out to the FBI
sourceContext.targetLine: because you're too big of a pussy to break up with her?
sourceContext.nextLine: I'll never tell, but, yes. Yes, I did.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 因为你太怂了，不敢跟她分手？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句用感情关系来戳对方怂，语气很损，也体现这剧里工程师社交能力常被拿来开刀。
zh-CN.usageNotes: break up with someone 是固定搭配。too ... to break up with her 这种结构很适合讽刺。

sourceContext.previousLine: I can't say that I am.
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, the science is actually pretty fascinating.
sourceContext.nextLine: Regular transfusions of the blood of a younger, physically fit donor

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 其实这里面的科学还挺让人着迷的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Gavin 一类人很爱用这种听起来理性又精英的词，把荒唐行为包装成高端兴趣。
zh-CN.usageNotes: fascinating 比 interesting 更强，通常表示“真的很想继续了解”。

sourceContext.previousLine: and Bryce is a picture of health. Just look at him.
sourceContext.targetLine: He looks like a Nazi propaganda poster.
sourceContext.nextLine: This is quite the baby shower.

definitions: 像纳粹宣传海报里的人
zh-CN.corePhrase: look like a Nazi propaganda poster
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他看起来简直像纳粹宣传海报上的形象。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是非常美式、非常毒的一种外貌类比，重点是高大、强壮、洗脑式完美。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 这种历史类比杀伤力很强，只适合理解，不适合乱用。

sourceContext.previousLine: Whoo!
sourceContext.targetLine: Uh... The fuck just happened?
sourceContext.nextLine: - Hey, uh, Bryce? - Yeah?

definitions: 刚刚到底发生了什么鬼
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 呃……刚刚那到底是什么鬼？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是人在强烈错愕时的即时反应，比 what happened 更带冲击感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: The fuck just happened? 很口语也很粗，适合惊愕吐槽，不适合正式场合。

sourceContext.previousLine: Well, the science is actually pretty fascinating.
sourceContext.targetLine: Regular transfusions of the blood of a younger, physically fit donor
sourceContext.nextLine: can significantly retard the aging process,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 定期输入来自更年轻、更健壮捐献者的血液。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这集把硅谷怪异 biohacking 推到很荒唐的程度。regular transfusions 听着就知道已经不只是养生，是近乎宗教的 obsession。
zh-CN.usageNotes: transfusion 是医疗硬词，常见搭配 blood transfusion。

sourceContext.previousLine: Well, the science is actually pretty fascinating.
sourceContext.targetLine: Regular transfusions of the blood of a younger, physically fit donor
sourceContext.nextLine: can significantly retard the aging process,

definitions: 身体状态很好的捐献者
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 定期输进一位更年轻、更健壮捐献者的血。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把 donor 都选成 physically fit，正体现这套荒诞逻辑背后的硅谷 elite obsession。
zh-CN.usageNotes: physically fit 常见于医疗、健身、招聘健康描述。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S04E06 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖 customer service、情绪降温和法律家庭语境表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: If you've taken one fucking selfie, I will find it.
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay. Guys, we've got to calm down. Okay?
sourceContext.nextLine: Look at this. This is not where we wanna be.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好了，大家都得先冷静下来，好吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 客服事故一来，第一步不是技术，而是别让团队先互相炸掉。
zh-CN.usageNotes: we've got to calm down 比 just calm down 更像在拉全队一起降温。

sourceContext.previousLine: and then I go ahead and do this?
sourceContext.targetLine: Shit! I mean, Dan's gonna find out.
sourceContext.nextLine: I mean, he punched Erlich in the face for when he did it to him

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 糟了，Dan 肯定会发现。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句不是普通得知消息，而是“事情要穿帮了”的恐慌。
zh-CN.usageNotes: someone's gonna find out 是秘密快暴露时的经典句型。

sourceContext.previousLine: Richard, this is no time for false humility.
sourceContext.targetLine: You're a catch. Just deal with it.
sourceContext.nextLine: Now, you need to break this thing off clean and clear,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你条件很好，接受这个事实吧。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句不是叫你处理任务，而是接受一个你不想面对的真相。
zh-CN.usageNotes: deal with it 很常见，但听起来容易显得不耐烦或嘴硬。

sourceContext.previousLine: I win!!
sourceContext.targetLine: Shut up, Jared.
sourceContext.nextLine: Last night.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 闭嘴，Jared。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 当关系足够熟又情绪上头时，shut up 会从玩笑瞬间变成真攻击。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 直接叫人 shut up 很冲，哪怕熟人之间也可能踩线。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah, it's just, it's the only place with room.
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay, uh, well, it's good to face your fears.
sourceContext.nextLine: I was scared of intruders until I had one of those in my room,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 嗯，直面自己的恐惧总归是件好事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这类劝法听起来像心理鸡汤，但在这集里更像硬逼别人去做不想做的事情。
zh-CN.usageNotes: face your fears 是很高频的鼓励表达，可真诚也可带一点站着说话不腰疼。

sourceContext.previousLine: Okay, first of all, my ex-wife is fucking the doctor that delivered my son
sourceContext.targetLine: and they're suing for full custody, so you're wrong.
sourceContext.nextLine: Second of all, this isn't a baby.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 而且他们现在在争完全抚养权，所以你错了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 美国家庭法律语境里，full custody 不是随便吵吵，是非常实打实的法律争夺。
zh-CN.usageNotes: custody 常用于离婚、子女抚养纠纷。sue for full custody 很常见。

sourceContext.previousLine: That was my idea, you were just standing there.
sourceContext.targetLine: Yeah, pulling it out of you like a newborn baby.
sourceContext.nextLine: Okay, but, uh, well, if a doctor pulls a baby out of a pregnant woman

definitions: 硬从你嘴里/身体里拽出来
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 对，就像把新生儿一样硬从你身体里拽出来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句非常夸张，用分娩比喻把人从 Richard 那里“逼出一句话”的痛苦感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: pull something out of someone 可指逼出信息、情绪、创意。

sourceContext.previousLine: That was the last wealthy guy in Silicon Valley that would even fucking talk to us.
sourceContext.targetLine: Maybe I'll give him a day to cool off, try him again tomorrow, something...
sourceContext.nextLine: Not so sure about that.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 也许我该给他一天时间冷静一下，明天再去试试。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 职场和关系冲突里，cool off 是非常实用的节奏管理：不是解决，而是先别继续加油。
zh-CN.usageNotes: give someone time to cool off 很地道，适合冲突后缓一缓。

sourceContext.previousLine: Stop trying to change the subject, Richard!
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay, well, uh, I had to go to Gavin because he owned the patent,
sourceContext.nextLine: which, as it turns out, he just signed over to me,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 嗯，我只能去找 Gavin，因为专利在他手里。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把技术理想重新拉回法律现实：谁 own the patent，谁就决定你能不能动。
zh-CN.usageNotes: own a patent / hold a patent 都常见。法律和创业语境里很关键。

sourceContext.previousLine: That was the last wealthy guy in Silicon Valley that would even fucking talk to us.
sourceContext.targetLine: Maybe I'll give him a day to cool off, try him again tomorrow, something...
sourceContext.nextLine: Not so sure about that.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 也许我该让他先冷静一天，明天再去试试。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句特别像客服和谈判场景里的 retry 思维：人和系统一样，今天不行明天再试。
zh-CN.usageNotes: try someone again later / tomorrow 很口语，表示改个时机再接触。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S04E07 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖 patent troll、法律威胁和成人风险语境表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: So, Erlich, good news.
sourceContext.targetLine: Keenan Feldspar signed his term sheet
sourceContext.nextLine: and we are now officially negotiating the finer points of his deal.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Keenan Feldspar 已经把他的 term sheet 签了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: term sheet 一签，局势就从“可能”变成“基本成了”。这句也让 Richard 感到时间被别人抢走了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: signed his term sheet 说明对方已经先一步锁定融资关系。

sourceContext.previousLine: It's probably not.
sourceContext.targetLine: But to find out, you're gonna have to fight him in court.
sourceContext.nextLine: For a good litigator, we're talking at least couple hundred grand.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 但你想知道答案，就得去法院跟他硬碰。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: patent troll 的麻烦不在于你是不是对，而在于你得花钱花时间去 court 里证明。
zh-CN.usageNotes: fight someone in court 很口语，但说的就是正式法律战。

sourceContext.previousLine: No, I am serious.
sourceContext.targetLine: I would like an STD check.
sourceContext.nextLine: What, did you graze your penis against a toilet seat?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我想做一个性病检查。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这集把 patent troll 的焦虑跟私生活焦虑并置，STD check 这句在美国语境里非常直接，也非常真实。
zh-CN.usageNotes: STD 是 sexually transmitted disease 的缩写。美国就医时直接这样说很正常。

sourceContext.previousLine: A female human.
sourceContext.targetLine: And I don't know this woman very well, and we didn't use a condom.
sourceContext.nextLine: Now listen, Richard, I wouldn't worry so much.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 而且我对那个女的也不熟，我们还没用安全套。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 美国成人对话里，直接说 condom 并不稀奇；真正的尴尬在于把不安全行为摊到台面上。
zh-CN.usageNotes: use a condom 是固定说法。didn't use a condom 会直接指向风险暴露。

sourceContext.previousLine: and in my earlier years, Chipotle made by not hiring me.
sourceContext.targetLine: All right. Don't hire me.
sourceContext.nextLine: But can you hack this finder's fee into 52 installments,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 行，那你们就别录用我。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 别人装作洒脱地说 don't hire me，常常是在先发制人，免得自己被真正拒绝。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Don't hire me 这种裸句很少见，正因为少见才显得情绪硬。

sourceContext.previousLine: You are an inch shorter than you were this time last year.
sourceContext.targetLine: You're shrinking, my friend.
sourceContext.nextLine: How's that possible?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 朋友，你在往下缩水。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里既可能是字面，也可能是在损对方气场和地位都在缩水。
zh-CN.usageNotes: shrinking 不只讲尺寸，也能带隐喻意味，表示人在退缩、变弱、变小。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yep.
sourceContext.targetLine: Guys like him buy a ton of super broad IP from failed startups at auction,
sourceContext.nextLine: and then threaten litigation against accused infringers, i.e. you.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 像他这种人会在拍卖里买一堆范围特别宽的 IP。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句点破 patent troll 的商业模式：不是靠做产品赚钱，而是靠囤一堆 broad IP 去收割别人。
zh-CN.usageNotes: broad IP / broad patent 指定义范围很宽、特别容易拿来告人的知识产权。

sourceContext.previousLine: "Sincerely, Stewart Burke."
sourceContext.targetLine: What, I thought we handled the patent stuff. We own it, right?
sourceContext.nextLine: Yeah. I'll send him a harshly worded cease and desist letter.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 什么？我还以为那些专利问题我们已经处理过了。那东西不是归我们了吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: patent stuff 这种模糊说法很真实，说明很多技术人知道它重要，但并不真正理解法律细节。
zh-CN.usageNotes: stuff 很口语，放在 patent 后面会显得说话人并不专业。

term.text: actually told quite a few people

sourceContext.previousLine: but I was under the impression that, um, I was going to be hired here.
sourceContext.targetLine: And I've actually told quite a few people that I was hired,
sourceContext.nextLine: so if I wasn't, then it would look like I was fired.

definitions: 我其实已经跟不少人说了
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 而且我其实已经跟不少人说过我被录用了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把 embarrassment 风险一下拉高，因为拒绝不再只影响本人，还要面对对外吹出去的话。
zh-CN.usageNotes: quite a few people 表示人数不少，但说得比较轻。很实用。

sourceContext.previousLine: We have an extra spot on the floor at Oracle Arena. You a hoops guy?
sourceContext.targetLine: Oops. Uh, sorry, you broke up. Could you, uh... Could you say that again?
sourceContext.nextLine: We have an extra spot on the floor tonight at Oracle Arena.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 哎呀，抱歉，你那边声音断了。能不能再说一遍？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 电话和视频通话里 broke up 是非常自然的网络问题说法，不是感情分手。
zh-CN.usageNotes: you broke up 常用于通话卡顿。别和 break up with someone 混。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S04E08 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖交易博弈、rollout 节奏和高压谈判表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: I see. Well, thoughts and prayers, obviously.
sourceContext.targetLine: But we'll still be ready to roll out in time for Hoolicon, right?
sourceContext.nextLine: Guys, hey, I'm not Gavin Belson.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 不过我们还是能赶在 Hoolicon 前按时推出，对吧？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 大型活动前的 rollout 不只是上线日期，而是整个公司节奏和外部预期的对撞点。
zh-CN.usageNotes: roll out in time for... 很适合描述配合展会、节日或发布会的上线。

sourceContext.previousLine: and since you're his keyman,
sourceContext.targetLine: maybe you could help set up a data storage deal
sourceContext.nextLine: like we had with Melcher.

zh-CN.corePhrase: set up a data storage deal
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 也许你能帮忙牵一个数据存储方面的 deal。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 技术路线一变，资源需求也变。data storage deal 一听就是要靠关系补基础设施。
zh-CN.usageNotes: storage deal / hosting deal 都带明显 B2B 技术商务色彩。

sourceContext.previousLine: We should be able to pull everything together by Monday?
sourceContext.targetLine: Is it cool if we hang out
sourceContext.nextLine: just to, like, play around with the VR some more?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们一起待会儿行吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在这集的高压谈判之外，这种轻飘飘的 hang out 反而更能看出人物社交里的试探。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Is it cool if we hang out? 很口语，比 Do you want to meet? 更轻。

sourceContext.previousLine: as we sink beneath the waves.
sourceContext.targetLine: But as poor as our odds are, I'm worried that if we turn down this offer,
sourceContext.nextLine: we will lose the rest of our crew,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 可就算我们的胜算再差，我还是担心如果我们把这个 offer 拒了，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里的张力很典型：明知条件不好，但拒绝它的风险可能更大。
zh-CN.usageNotes: turn down an offer 是商业和求职都很常见的表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: Also, and I know this might piss you off to some degree...
sourceContext.targetLine: I have decided to make a deal with Keenan Feldspar.
sourceContext.nextLine: What? Why?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我已经决定要跟 Keenan Feldspar 做这笔交易。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 一旦说 make a deal with 某人，通常说明原则和不适感都已经被现实压过去了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: make a deal with someone 很口语，也比 sign an agreement 更带人情和博弈感。

sourceContext.previousLine: Really. I just...
sourceContext.targetLine: If I don't calm down, I will...
sourceContext.nextLine: I will take you up on that.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 如果我不先冷静下来，我就要……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句停在句中，比说完整更有张力，像人在真的快失控边缘硬刹车。
zh-CN.usageNotes: If I don't calm down... 很适合表达情绪快爆掉之前的自我提醒。

sourceContext.previousLine: tries to oust me from Bream-Hall,
sourceContext.targetLine: Keenan Feldspar can void the deal and walk.
sourceContext.nextLine: So, basically, this new title just means

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Keenan Feldspar 随时可以让这笔 deal 作废然后直接走人。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句点出弱势方最怕的事：你已经投入情绪和筹码，但对方还能随时掀桌。
zh-CN.usageNotes: void the deal 很法务，也很商战。比 cancel 更像正式失效。

sourceContext.previousLine: tries to oust me from Bream-Hall,
sourceContext.targetLine: Keenan Feldspar can void the deal and walk.
sourceContext.nextLine: So, basically, this new title just means

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Keenan Feldspar 可以让交易失效，然后直接甩手走人。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: walk 在商业谈判里单独用，意思不是散步，而是“走人、不玩了”。短得很狠。
zh-CN.usageNotes: walk away 更完整；单说 walk 在 deal 语境里也常成立。

sourceContext.previousLine: Where have you been? We've been trying to close your deal.
sourceContext.targetLine: Monica and Laurie were really fighting me on one final deal point
sourceContext.nextLine: and I was getting bummed,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Monica 和 Laurie 在最后一个 deal point 上跟我缠得特别厉害。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 大 deal 往往不是整体不行，而是卡在一个让人寸步不让的细节点上。
zh-CN.usageNotes: deal point 是谈判里很实用的词，指某一个具体条款或争议点。

sourceContext.previousLine: It's freezing in here.
sourceContext.targetLine: Tsk, tsk, Richard. Negativity.
sourceContext.nextLine: Have you seen Keenan's demo?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 啧啧，Richard，又开始负能量了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Keenan 这种人会把正常担忧直接包装成 negativity，从而占据道德高地。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Negativity 单独拎出来说很像轻蔑点评，意思是“你又在散发负面情绪”。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S04E09 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖展会营销、增长手段和被迫 adoption 表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: Not to me.
sourceContext.targetLine: Look, we set up a bunch of these at Hoolicon.
sourceContext.nextLine: We position ourselves between everyone's devices and the real WiFi.

zh-CN.corePhrase: set up a bunch of these
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你看，这些东西我们在 Hoolicon 已经搭了好多套。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: trade show 现场的 set up 不是抽象规划，而是非常具体的展位、物料和演示装置。
zh-CN.usageNotes: set up booths / demos / stations 都很自然。

sourceContext.previousLine: I will not help you on this mission.
sourceContext.targetLine: Richard, we always said we didn't want to end up like Hooli.
sourceContext.nextLine: How are we like Hooli?

definitions: 最后变成 Hooli 那样
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Richard，我们一直都说过，我们不想最后变成 Hooli 那样。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是价值观拷问: 为了增长和成功，你会不会变成自己最讨厌的公司。
zh-CN.usageNotes: end up like someone/something 很适合表达“不想活成那样”的警醒。

sourceContext.previousLine: And... And we do as well. Uh...
sourceContext.targetLine: You know, why don't you go check out what the bathroom situation's like?
sourceContext.nextLine: Yes.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 要不你去看看那边厕所情况到底怎么样？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 展会现场连厕所都能成为运营问题，说明场面已经乱到每个细节都得有人盯。
zh-CN.usageNotes: check out what ... is like 是很口语的探查句型。

sourceContext.previousLine: And... And we do as well. Uh...
sourceContext.targetLine: You know, why don't you go check out what the bathroom situation's like?
sourceContext.nextLine: Yes.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你去看看厕所那边现在到底是什么情况。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: situation 放在 bathroom 后面会显得事情已经不只是“有没有厕所”，而是成了一个现场管理问题。
zh-CN.usageNotes: the X situation 是很常见的模糊但真实的口语说法。

sourceContext.previousLine: Look, he turned around. I had like a second, okay?
sourceContext.targetLine: It's the best I could come up with.
sourceContext.nextLine: PoopFare?

definitions: 我能想出来的最好版本
zh-CN.corePhrase: the best I could come up with
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这已经是我能想出来最好的方案了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在资源紧、时间紧的情况下，这句很像创作者和运营人的真实辩解。
zh-CN.usageNotes: the best I could come up with 是很自然的解释句，适合方案不完美时使用。

sourceContext.previousLine: Look me in the eyes, and you name our undoing.
sourceContext.targetLine: Hey, come on. Calm down now.
sourceContext.nextLine: PoopFare? You reckless child.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 哎，行了，先冷静下来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 加个 now 会让 calm down 更像现场拦人别继续升级。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Calm down now 比单独 calm down 多一点即时制止意味。

sourceContext.previousLine: A veritable who's who of nobodies.
sourceContext.targetLine: Richard, we've tried marketing at trade shows.
sourceContext.nextLine: We suck at it.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Richard，我们不是没试过在行业展会上做营销。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: trade show 在美国创业和企业市场里非常常见，既是获客渠道，也是品牌表演场。
zh-CN.usageNotes: trade show 比 expo 更偏行业和商务语境。

sourceContext.previousLine: Think of it more as, um, forced adoption through aggressive guerrilla marketing.
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, as a product of forced adoption, I can assure you, there are consequences.
sourceContext.nextLine: - I like it. - Well, hold On.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 作为一个被迫采用这种东西的人，我可以告诉你，后果是有的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句很妙，它把“用户用了”跟“用户愿意用”分得很开。forced adoption 说的是被逼着接受。
zh-CN.usageNotes: adoption 在产品里通常是好词，但 forced adoption 一下就变成负面。

sourceContext.previousLine: If we have to bend a few rules here and there...
sourceContext.targetLine: I mean, it's all for the greater good, is it not?
sourceContext.nextLine: Gavin Belson started out with lofty goals, too,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我的意思是，这一切不都是为了更大的整体利益吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 一旦有人开始拿 greater good 当理由，往往说明他准备替某些不太体面的手段正名。
zh-CN.usageNotes: for the greater good 很常见，但带明显道德包装色彩。

sourceContext.previousLine: people are just gonna delete the app as soon as the conference is over.
sourceContext.targetLine: People don't delete apps. I'm telling you. Get your phones out, right now.
sourceContext.nextLine: Uh, Hipstamatic. Vine, may she rest in peace.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 人们是不会删 app 的，我告诉你。现在把手机都拿出来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句体现了增长黑暗面：只要装上了，很多人懒得删，你就能把安装量算成胜利。
zh-CN.usageNotes: delete apps / uninstall apps 都能用；delete apps 更口语。

summary.zh-CN: 基于 S04E10 真实字幕生成，重点覆盖上线故障、临场冲突和 steamroll 式压人表达。

sourceContext.previousLine: if we don't get this shit to Standford
sourceContext.targetLine: and all set up by 9:00 a.m.,
sourceContext.nextLine: we are fucked!

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 而且最晚到上午九点前全部要布置好。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 服务器事故前夜，这句先把节奏拉满：不仅要做，还要在明确时间点前完成。
zh-CN.usageNotes: all set up by + 时间点 很适合活动、上线、搬运协调。

sourceContext.previousLine: I'm sorry, we have to... We have to go. Bye.
sourceContext.targetLine: Back up, back up. Thank you.
sourceContext.nextLine: Big Head. Hey, I need you to meet me at the loading dock

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 往后退，往后退一点，谢谢。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这种连说两遍的 back up 很像现场人多事杂时的空间控制。
zh-CN.usageNotes: back up 可用于现场指挥，也可讲数据备份。别混。

sourceContext.previousLine: Come on, guys, we don't have time for this.
sourceContext.targetLine: You were just going to show up and fucking steamroll him?
sourceContext.nextLine: That was your whole plan? He's your oldest friend,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你原本就打算这么直接过去，然后一路把他碾过去？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是在骂 Richard 做事太莽：没有布局，没有准备，只想靠现场压过去。
zh-CN.usageNotes: show up and do something 常带“什么都不准备就直接上”的味道。

sourceContext.previousLine: Come on, guys, we don't have time for this.
sourceContext.targetLine: You were just going to show up and fucking steamroll him?
sourceContext.nextLine: That was your whole plan? He's your oldest friend,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你是打算直接过去把他一路压过去吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: steamroll 不是普通说服，而是靠气势和力量把别人碾平。很适合形容谈判中的霸凌推进。
zh-CN.usageNotes: steamroll someone / steamroll a process 都很常见，带很强压迫感。

sourceContext.previousLine: I'm sorry.
sourceContext.targetLine: What the fuck is this?
sourceContext.nextLine: I write three letters every time I start a job...

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这他妈到底是什么情况？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 系统或现场一出大故障，这种第一反应比任何技术描述都更真实。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 极口语、极粗。适合情绪爆炸时理解，不适合随便对人用。

sourceContext.previousLine: all had our code on them.
sourceContext.targetLine: Richard, these are real people
sourceContext.nextLine: with real crotches, and they're burning.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Richard，这些可都是真人，不是系统里的抽象数字。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Anton 和算法思维的问题之一，就是容易把用户当流量。这里有人强行把人性拉回现场。
zh-CN.usageNotes: real people 常用于提醒别人别把事情只当指标或模型。

sourceContext.previousLine: You can't leave.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Don't beg, Richard. - I'm not.
sourceContext.nextLine: According to the employee handbook,

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 别求他，Richard。我没有。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句不只是面子问题，而是权力关系问题：一旦 beg，就等于承认自己在下位。
zh-CN.usageNotes: beg 可真是乞求，也可比喻低姿态讨要。

sourceContext.previousLine: Wait. We have a server farm in the garage, right?
sourceContext.targetLine: Now, don't you think if Anton could've saved our ass,
sourceContext.nextLine: I would've suggested it by now?

definitions: 救我们一命；替我们收残局
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 现在你难道不觉得，要是 Anton 真能救我们这一把的话，
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 技术团队在极端压力下说 save our ass，很符合灾难现场气氛。
zh-CN.usageNotes: save my/our ass 是很高频粗口化表达，表示从大麻烦里捞出来。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Is that the secret on your phone? - Fuck you.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Fuck you! Do you raw-dog it? - Guys. Guys. Guys!
sourceContext.nextLine: Or do you put a condom on your tiny penis and fuck it in safe mode?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 滚啊！你平时都直接不做防护的吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是非常粗的成人化比喻，拿来形容技术或生活里什么都不垫就硬上。
zh-CN.usageNotes: raw-dog 在现代口语里常被扩大成“完全不做缓冲/不带任何辅助地硬扛”。非常口语。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah, we've all been busy, so.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Oh yeah? - Yeah. Hey, listen, Big Head,
sourceContext.nextLine: do you think I could get your Stanford login

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 哦是吗？对。诶，Big Head，你听我说。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 虽然是角色外号，但这句更值得学的是 Hey, listen 这种拦人进入正题的口语节奏。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Hey, listen 是很常见的抢话/转话题起手式。这里保留是因为 targetLine 里最可迁移的是说话节奏，不是人名本身。
